npp-plugins.description:
URL Encoder and Decoder plug-in for Notepad++ which helps to encode/decode URLs within Notepad++.

How it works:
1. Configure settings from menu (Plugins > URL Plugin > URL Plugin Settings).
2. Select text/URL which you want to encode/decode.
3. Perform your task via Plugins > URL Plugin > Encode/Decode URL.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/notepad-plus-plus-mac/nppURLPlugin
npp-plugins.author: Rajendra Singh

npp-plugins.description:
Compare two files side by side with full diff highlighting, alignment, and navigation.

Features:
- File and selection comparison with Myers diff algorithm
- Changed/added/removed/moved line detection with color highlighting
- Character-level diff highlighting within changed lines
- Diff block navigation (Next/Previous/First/Last)
- Vertical scroll synchronization
- Compare summary statistics

Coming soon:
- Find Unique Lines
- Diff since last Save
- Compare file/selection to Clipboard
- SVN Diff / Git Diff
- Auto Re-Compare on Change

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/notepad-plus-plus-mac/ComparePlus
npp-plugins.author: Pavel Nedev

npp-plugins.description:
IndentByFold uses Scintilla fold points to indent code. Language-independent — works with any language that has fold points.

Features:
- Reindent File command (entire document)
- Auto-indent on Enter based on fold levels
- Auto-dedent on closing constructs (e.g. })

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/notepad-plus-plus-mac/indentbyfold
npp-plugins.author: Ben Bluemel, Frank Fesevur

Reverses the order of selected lines or all lines in the document.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
Toggle visibility of fold underlines (green horizontal lines under collapsed code blocks). Also provides Collapse/Uncollapse current fold level commands.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/notepad-plus-plus-mac/FoldingLineHider

npp-plugins.description:
Pipe selected text through user-defined external programs. Supports macros, input/output files, async execution. Includes 7 built-in commands (sort, compress, base64) and a Java demo.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/notepad-plus-plus-mac/pork2sausage
npp-plugins.author: Don HO

npp-plugins.description:
Opens the system hosts file (/etc/hosts) in a new Notepad++ tab with a single menu click. Adds a "Plugins > Open Host file > Open" menu item. Handy for quickly inspecting or editing host overrides.

Note: /etc/hosts is root-owned on macOS, so saving edits requires elevating privileges separately.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/notepad-plus-plus-mac/NppPluginOpenHost
npp-plugins.author: jejemorg

npp-plugins.description:
Export the current document (or a selection) to RTF or HTML with full syntax highlighting preserved — fonts, colors, bold/italic/underline per Scintilla style. Five menu items:

- Export to RTF (save to file)
- Export to HTML (save to file)
- Copy RTF to clipboard
- Copy HTML to clipboard
- Copy all formats to clipboard

Clipboard commands put the rich flavor (public.rtf / public.html) for rich paste targets (Mail, Word, TextEdit Rich) and the rendered source markup as the plain-text fallback so pasting into a plain-text target (another Notepad++ tab, Terminal) shows the same bytes the file export would write. "Copy all" puts both rich flavors plus a concatenated HTML + RTF + plain text blob.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/notepad-plus-plus-mac/NPP_ExportPlugin
npp-plugins.author: Christian Grasser
